content.problemStatement:
In India, every individual is charged with income tax on the total income each year. This tax is applied to specific ranges of income, which are called income tax slabs. The slabs of income tax keep changing from year to year. This fiscal year (2020-21), the tax slabs and their respective tax rates are as follows:

Total income (in rupees)Tax rateup to Rs. 250,0000%from Rs. 250,001 to Rs. 500,0005%from Rs. 500,001 to Rs. 750,00010%from Rs. 750,001 to Rs. 1,000,00015%from Rs. 1,000,001 to Rs. 1,250,00020%from Rs. 1,250,001 to Rs. 1,500,00025%above Rs. 1,500,00030%See the sample explanation for details on how the income tax is calculated.

You are given Chef's total income: NNN rupees (Rs.). Find his net income. The net income is calculated by subtracting the total tax (also called tax reduction) from the total income. Note that you do not need to worry about any other kind of tax reductions, only the one described above.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — Chef's net income.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We know that the total income is Rs. 666 lakh (111 lakh rupees = 10510^5105 rupees). The total tax for each slab is calculated as follows:

Up to 2.52.52.5 lakh, the tax is Rs. 000, since the tax rate is 000 percent.
From above Rs. 2.52.52.5 lakh to Rs. 555 lakh, the tax rate is 555 percent. Therefore, this tax is 0.05⋅(500,000−250,000)0.05 \cdot (500,000-250,000)0.05⋅(500,000−250,000), which is Rs. 12,50012,50012,500.
From above Rs. 555 lakh to Rs. 666 lakh, the tax rate is 101010 percent. Therefore, this tax is 0.10⋅(600,000−500,000)0.10 \cdot (600,000-500,000)0.10⋅(600,000−500,000), which is Rs. 10,00010,00010,000.
Summing them up, we get that the total tax on Chef's whole income is Rs. 22,50022,50022,500. Since the net income is the total income minus the tax reduction, it is Rs. 600,000600,000600,000 minus Rs. 22,50022,50022,500, which is Rs. 577,500577,500577,500.

Example case 2: For income up to Rs. 2.52.52.5 lakh, we have no tax, so the net income is the same as the total income: Rs. 2.52.52.5 lakh.
